title: heptapleurum violeum
intro: Heptapleurum violeum, also known as the Viola-leaved Cheirostylis or simply the Cheirostylis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cypripediaceae. This orchid species is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, where it is often found growing in shaded areas with high humidity. Heptapleurum violeum has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, with its health benefits attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, and it is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for herbal use.
